segments.title: Castles as instruments of sea power
segments.summary: Clarke previews a study of the Welsh castle ring that treats access to the sea as a central influence on fortification strategy and design.
segments.body: Clarke closes the announcement video by previewing a Long Patrol on the ring of stone castles used to pacify Wales. He frames these fortifications as maritime as well as land defenses, asking how the sea shaped their purpose and design. The study will trace this strategic logic back to Roman precedents and forward through the Welsh castle-building program. He then extends the same question into science fiction by comparing bases on moons, large satellites, and asteroids with installations on a planet's surface. This brief passage establishes the sea-power lens that the longer castle series will explore.

evidence.note: Clarke announces a castle series on the maritime logic of fortifications used to pacify Wales, with Roman and science-fiction comparisons.
